series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence pertaining to the
formulation, planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or
redefinition of the services, or projects of
an agency and the administrative
regulations, policies, and procedures that
govern them.

Non-administrative incoming/outgoing
and internal correspondence, in any
media, pertaining to or arising from the
routine operations of the policies,
programs, services, or projects of an
agency.

Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions. Some examples of
transitory information, which can be in
any medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard
copy, etc.) are routine messages;
telephone message notifications; internal
meeting notices; routing slips; incoming
letters or memoranda of transmittal that
add nothing of substance to enclosures;
and similar routine information used for
communication, but not for the
documentation, of a specific agency
transaction.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
The disposal of transitory
information need not be
documented in records disposition
logs (R1-02).

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence (including by email or
online contact form) or packages by the
U.S. Postal Service or by courier.

Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or e-mail address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records, purchased with state funds or
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
Note: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records
of executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
Archives Department when these
records have met their retention
periods. Caution: A record of this
type purchased with personal
funds, but used by a Bar employee
or representative to document his
or her work activities may be
subject to this retention period.

series_description:
Print or electronic masters of original
version and all subsequent revisions to
agency forms, including any associated
design or modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Data resident on hard drives that make
use of user-specific information
transmitted by the Web server onto the
user's computer so that the information
might be available for later access by itself
or other servers.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
AV = upon browser exit; this does
not apply to such records that may
have been captured or retained for
investigative or human resource
actions and as such are subject to
further retention. The disposal of
Internet cookies need not be
documented in records disposition
logs (R1-02).

series_description:
A record of the documents visited during
an Internet session that allows users to
access previously visited pages more
quickly or to generate a record of usage of
a state-owned computer.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of history files need
not be documented records
disposition logs (R1-02).

series_title:
Software Registration,
Warranties and License
Agreements
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years
comments:
Registrations, warranties, and
license agreements of software not
issued or maintained by the State
Bar of Texas IT Department must
be maintained by the department
that purchased the product.

series_description:
Agreements or contracts between the
agency and equipment vendor to provide
maintenance service for equipment.
Includes requests for installation, moves,

etc.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years
Life of Asset

series_description:
List of agency or department contracts,
leases, and agreements including general
obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction contracts.

Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, successful and unsuccessful
bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, successful and unsuccessful
bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations for
unsuccessful bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, successful and unsuccessful
bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or before August
31, 2015.

series_description:

or after 9/1/2015
series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide. Executed, renewed, or amended on
or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
SEE related item number
A1-17 for Bid Documentation.
SEE item number F1-05
for building construction
contracts and item number
A1-16 for contract logs.

series_title:

or before 8/31/2015
series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide. Executed, renewed, or amended on
or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:

A1-19 for Bid Documentation.
SEE item number F1-05
for building construction
contracts and item number
A1-16 for contract logs.

Based on Strategic Plan. Final performance

comments:
Caution: The FE + 3 retention

Government Code
541.048. State Bar
Board Policy Manual,
1.12.05, 1.15.04,
1.15.05

series_title:
Board of Directors
Meeting Agenda and
Minutes
series_description:
Official agenda and minutes of the State
Bar of Texas Board of Directors and Board
of Directors Committees, including the
Executive Committee. Includes agendas,
minutes, and exhibits.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_title:
Board of Directors
Meetings Supporting
Documentation
series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of the
State Bar of Texas Board of Directors and
Board of Directors Committees, including
the Executive Committee. Includes
meeting packet items, documentation for
agenda items, meeting handouts, and
documents sent in advance of meetings
for briefing purposes, some of which may
not be submitted at an actual meeting.

series_title:
Board of Directors Open
Meeting Audiovisual
Recordings
series_description:
Audio or videotapes of open meetings of
the State Bar of Texas Board of Directors
and Board of Directors Committees,
including the Executive Committee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days

series_title:
Board of Directors Closed
Meeting Certified Agendas
and Audiovisual
Recordings
series_description:
Certified agendas or tape recordings of the
State Bar of Texas Board of Directors and
Board of Directors Committees, including
the Executive Committee
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Notes Taken to Prepare
Board of Directors
Meeting Minutes
series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of the
State Bar of Texas Board of Directors and
Board of Directors Committees, including
the Executive Committee from which
written minutes are prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days

series_title:
State Bar Leadership
Speeches, Papers, and
Presentations
series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered by
State Bar leadership (officers, Board
members, Executive Director) in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
At-Large Director
Nomination and Selection
Records - Selected
series_description:
Nomination and selection records for At-
Large Directors (previously "Minority
Directors") chosen to serve on the State
Bar of Texas Board of Directors.
Nomination packet (resume, letter of
interest, letters of recommendation),
nomination tracking spreadsheet,
committee correspondence, voting survey.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
At-Large Director
Nomination and Selection
Records - Not Selected
series_description:
Nomination and selection records for At-
Large Directors (previously "Minority
Directors") not chosen to serve on the
State Bar of Texas Board of Directors.
Nomination packet (resume, letter of
interest, letters of recommendation),
nomination tracking spreadsheet,
committee correspondence, voting survey.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Tracks both CAAP mediation/dispute
resolution and Grievance Hotline activity.
Includes call tracking form, client and
attorney information, and incident
information. Used to generate case
management notes.

Database-generated list of all actions
taken on a given case.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 months
comments:
Note: These records are
confidential and nontransferrable
to the CDC.

series_description:
Initiates CAAP dispute resolution process.
Includes CAAP disclaimer and waiver of
attorney-client privilege. Filed in Client
File.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 months
comments:
Note: These records are
confidential and nontransferrable
to the CDC.

series_description:
Official agenda and minutes of
Commission for Lawyer Discipline
meetings. Includes any exhibits to the
minutes.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of the
Commission for Lawyer Discipline.
Includes meeting packets, memos of
attorney's recommendations to the
commission, administrative information,
financials, and statistical reports.
Supporting documentation may be
distributed at the meeting or sent in
advance of meetings for briefing purposes.

series_title:
CFLD Reports to the State
Bar of Texas Board of
Directors
series_description:
Quarterly disciplinary system reports
submitted to the State Bar of Texas Board
of Directors.
comments:
Note: official copy appears in the
Permanent Minutes of the State
Bar of Texas Board of Directors.

series_description:
Form used by State Bar Board of Directors
member to nominate prospective
Grievance Committee members. Gathers
information on area of practice, firm size,
basic identification information, and
background check release.
legal_citation:
Texas Disciplinary
Rules of Professional
Conduct, Rule 2.03

series_description:
Client application (and associated
evidence?) for financial relief from the
Client Security Fund following
demonstration of theft or failure to refund
unearned fees through the griefance
process
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 15 years

series_description:
Notes and phone records from Ethics
Helpline calls handled by Ethics Attorneys.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Documentation of the Chief Disciplinary
Counsel's involvement in the cessation of
a law practice in the event an attorney
dies, disappears, or becomes
incapacitated, leaving a law practice for
which no other attorney licensed to
practice in Texas has agreed to assume
responsibility. May include, but is not
limited to, correspondence, investigation
records, and attorney work product.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of the
Chief Disciplinary Counsel's office in
anticipation of or in the adjudication of a
lawsuit not related to an attorney
discipline grievance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Report completed for every grievance case
classifying the grievance as an inquiry
(dismissed) or a complaint (not dismissed)
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Documentation of a grievance filed and
classified as an inquiry, i.e. dismissed.
Includes Grievance Intake records
(Grievance Form and supporting
documentation). Does not include
Screening Action Report (C2-01).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 180 days
comments:
The content of inquiry files is
confidential, pursuant to Rule 2.16
T.R.D.P. A

series_description:
Report created following investigation to
determine whether there is Just Cause.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Confidential document, pursuant to
Rule 2.16 T.R.D.P.

series_description:
Report on outcome of Summary
Disposition Panel (SDP) hearing, which is
held after No Just Cause determination for
SDP to vote on whether to dismiss or
proceed with the complaint.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Confidential document, pursuant to
Rule 2.16 T.R.D.P.

series_description:
Documentation of a grievance in which No
Just Cause is determined. Includes records
from Grievance Intake (Grievance Form
and supporting documentation), as well as
investigation records, appeal notifications,
BODA decision, responses, rebuttals,
additional supporting documentation,
hearing notifications, and Grievance
Committee panel attendance report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 180 days

series_title:
Grievance Litigation Files -
No Sanction, Non-Suits,
Dismissals
series_description:
Litigation file for a grievance with the
outcome of no sanction, non-suit, or
dismissal by Commission for Lawyer
Discipline (CFLD), in the event new
information comes to light that
determines there is no case, despite an
earlier Just Cause determination or in the
case of a Grievance Referral Program
(GRP) dismissal. In addition to litigation
records, includes everything from
Grievance Intake, investigation, and
Election Letter/Notification (in which
respondent is informed of Just Cause
determination and given the opportunity
to elect where the case will be heard).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Portions of these records may be
confidential, pursuant to Rule 2.16
T.R.D.P.

series_title:
Grievance Litigation Files -
Disbarments and
Resignations in Lieu of
Discipline
series_description:
Litigation file for a grievance with the
outcome of disbarment or resignation in
lieu of discipline. Includes petition, public
judgment, compliance documentation,
and appeals documentation, as well as
everything from Grievance Intake,
investigation, and Election
Letter/Notification.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Portions of these records may be
confidential, pursuant to Rule 2.16
T.R.D.P.

series_title:
Grievance Litigation Files -
Sanctions (except
disbarments and
resignations in lieu of
discipline)
series_description:
Litigation file for a grievance with the
outcome of sanctions other than
disbarment or resignation in lieu of
discipline, i.e. suspension or reprimand.
Includes petition, public judgment,
compliance documentation, and appeals
documentation, as well as everything from
Grievance Intake, investigation, and
Election Letter/Notification.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Portions of these records may be
confidential, pursuant to Rule 2.16
T.R.D.P.

series_description:
Litigation files for a grievance resulting in a
disability determination notification/order
reported to the Chief Disciplinary Counsel.
Includes pleadings, petitions, orders, case
information, and reinstatement records
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
"Closing Packet" for final public judgment.
Contains judgment, disciplinary petition,
findings of fact, and compliance
documentation (including payment
receipts)
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
"Closing Packet" for final private
judgment. Contains judgment, disciplinary
petition, findings of fact, and compliance
documentation (including payment
receipts)
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
These records are confidential,
pursuant to Rule 2.16 T.R.D.P.

series_description:
Records documenting terms of final
judgment and compliance with those
terms. Include judgment, compliance
demand letters, rehabilitation
requirements, affadavits for license,
correspondence, and copies of checks
related to final judgment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting compliance with
terms of final judgment, such as copies of
checks received and bar card affidavits.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Portions of these records may be
confidential, pursuant to Rule 2.16
TRDP

series_description:
Grievance Referral Program compliance
records, including rehabilitation terms &
conditions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Portions of these records may be
confidential, pursuant to Rule 2.16
TRDP

series_description:
Questionnaires or surveys distributed to
complainant at final outcome of grievance,
whether classified as inquiry (intake),
given no just cause determination
(upgrade), or taken through litigation.
Source of statistics for "Grievance
Oversight Report," submitted to Grievance
Oversight Committee.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Only the final, certified results for
Referenda and Bar Leadership elections.

series_description:
Includes both daily and final activity
reports on ballot returns throughout
election process. Does not include final
official certified results.

Elections, referenda, and judicial poll
ballots.

Includes only the final official certified poll
results.

series_description:

Data used to produce
election/referendum and judicial poll
activity reports.

Includes both daily and final activity
reports on ballot returns throughout
judicial poll process. Does not include final
official certified results.

Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency. Includes Supreme
Court Orders.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings

and changes. The records may be

including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

Agency Rules, Policies, and

series_description:
Working files used in the development of

or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects.

Rules, Policies, and Procedures -
Final.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes State Bar Act, State Bar
Rules, Texas Disciplinary Rules of
Professional Conduct, Texas Rules of
Disciplinary Procedure, and Board Policy
Manual, as well as any supplemental
procedures governing the Bar's regulatory
responsibilities, such as Advertising
Review and Minimum Continuing Legal
Education (MCLE).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
SEE item number E2-03, Agency
Rules, Policies, and Procedures -
Working Files.

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.

series_description:
Includes notes of interviews with

selection process.

history checks. See item number H2-
03.

series_title:
Employment Eligibility,
Documentation or
Verification of
series_description: Federal reporting form (INS I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Caution: Federal regulation
requires that INS 1-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever is later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for the 3 year retention
period.

series_description:
Contains posting number, job title,
department information, signatures, offer
date, date of orientation, and who made
the offer.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
The State Bar of Texas does not accept
unsolicited resumes.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
SEE item number H1-05,
Employment Selection Records, for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee including pay

number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee

family information open or confidential
under the Public Information Act,
Government Code §552.024.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

SEE item number H3-03, Former
Employee Verification Records

from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas to
its employees.

comments:
Caution: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the

item number P2-06, Employee
Deduction Authorizations.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection of available benefit options
other than insurance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Caution: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number P2-06, Employee
Deduction Authorizations.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Caution: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U.
S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number L3-01, Litigation Files.

series_description:
Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and
are imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Caution: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action, all
documentation from this series
used to support disciplinary action
must be retained for the the
minimum retention period
described by item number H2-09,
Personnel Disciplinary Action
Documentation

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure
to improve performance or conduct
following imposition of corrective action.
May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons
for failure to give written notice of
resignation. May also be used to
document evidence of employee self-
improvement efforts, as well as favorable
and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Requests and
Authorizations to Engage
in Reimbursable Education
and Training
series_description:
Requests and authorizations to attend
college classes or equivalent training for
which the expenses of the employee are
defrayed or reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
Note that Requests and
Authorizations for Out of State
Travel (M1-15) are retained by
Department Directors.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts, test
scores, or similar records documenting the
training, testing, or continuing education

United States, Code
of Federal
Regulations. 29 CFR
1904.33.

computer programs.

Automated processing files that fall
under records series listed

accordance with most restrictive
applicable retention schedule.

accordance with most restrictive
applicable retention schedule.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical

documentation describing how a

and output specifications, and system flow
charts; program descriptions and
documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, and other records that
document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system such as a data element dictionary,
file layout, code book or table, and other
records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.
